Attached to the foregoing Act is the following veto message
signed by the Governor of Kentucky , March 24, 1924 :

396 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
House Bill No. 535 having been delivered in my office on
March ,13,1924 , and having been examined by me ,I disapprove of
Sub -section D of Section 37 of said House Bill No. 535 , appro-
priating to the University of Kentucky the sum of Fifteen Thou-
sand ($15,000.00) Dollars for the purpose of investigating the
prevention of diseases of tobacco , and other matters relating
thereto , because , in my judgment , the Experimental Station of
the University of Kentucky is and has been amply provided for
and has sufficient funds to make any investigations it deems ad-
visable to make in the interest of Agriculture .
In view of the present financial condition of the State on the
one hand , and the fact that the need is not imperative on the
other , I feel impelled to disapprove of sub -section F , of Section
37 , of House Bill 535 , appropriating to the University of Ken-
tucky Thirty Thousand ($30,000.00) Dollars for the completion
of a chemistry building .
I also disapprove of Sub -section A of Section 38 of House Bill
535 , relative to the additional Ten Thousand ($10,000.00) Dollars
added to the amount appropriated by the Budget Commission by
way of an amendment by the State Legislature , and approve of
the Sixty Thousand ($60,000.00) Dollars recommended and ap-
propriated by the Budget Commission .
I disapprove of all of the foregoing sub -sections of House Bill
No. 535 , in addition to the reasons hereinbefore set out , because
the financial condition of the State does not , at this time , permit
these expenditures , and I therefore veto the sub -sections of the
sections hereinbefore mentioned .
I also disapprove of Sub -section B of Section 42 of House Bill
535 , which appropriates Seventeen Thousand Five Hundred
($17,500.00 ) Dollars to the Department of the State Geologist for
co-operative work with the United States Geological Survey ,
other provision having been made for this Department by a sep-
arate Act of the recent Legislature . I, therefore , veto the above
subsection B of Section 42 of House Bill 535 .
All of the other provisions and appropriations contained in
House Bill No. 535 are hereby approved .
W. J. FIELDS , Governor of Kentucky .
March 24 , 1924 .

CHAPTER 117 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation ; fixing the annual
State rate on the shares of stock of national banks , State
banks and trust companies ; prescribing by whom and to whom
the taxes shall be paid and limiting the local rates of taxation
thereon for county , city, town and school purposes , and pro-
viding for the distribution of the fund derived from such taxa-
tion, repealing and re-enacting , as amended , Section 4092 of

416 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's edition, 1915 ; providing for a ref-
erendum if desired , and repealing all laws or parts of laws in
conflict herewith .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssembly oftheCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That section4092 of the Kentucky Statutes,
Carroll'sedition,1915,be and thesame ishereby re-
pealed and in lieu thereof that said sectionbe re-
enacted so as to read as follows :
(a) An annual tax of fiftycents (50 cents) on
each one hundred dollars of their fair cash value is
hereby imposed upon the shares of stock of State
banks and trustcompanies,incorporatedunder the
laws of this Commonwealth , and of national banks
doing businesstherein, and such tax shallbe paid
to the sheriffof the county annuallyby such banks
and trustcompanies for and on behalf of the owners
of such shares of stock; such tax shallbe for State
purposes only, and in lieuof allother taxesupon
said sharesby the State,or any county,city,town ,or
other taxing district.
(b) Except that the county in which the said
bank or trust company islocated may impose a tax
of not exceedingtwenty cents(20 cents) on the one
hundred dollars of the fair cash value of such
shares ; and
(c) Except that the cityor town in which said
bank or trustcompany is located may impose a tax
of not exceedingtwenty cents(20 cents) on the one
hundred dollars of the fair cash value of such
shares ; and
(d ) Except thatthe tax of not exceedingforty
cents(40 cents) on the one hundred dollarsof the
fair cash value of such shares may be imposed for
schoolpurposes by the regularlyconstitutedauthor-
itieslevyingsuchtax or taxesinthedistrictinwhich

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 417
suchbank ortrustcompany islocated;providedthat
when more than one levy is made for schoolpur-
poses in the same year upon the same property,the
said authorizedlevy, but not exceedingfortycents
(40 cents) in the aggregate,shallbe distributedbe-
tween said leviesfor schoolpurposes in the propor-
tionwhich the rate of each bears to the aggregate of
all said rates .
§2 . In the event a referendum is desired upon
thisact,then such referendum shallbe conducted in
allthingsin the manner providedby chaptereight
oftheActs oftheGeneralAssembly of one thousand
nine hundred and seventeen .
§3 . If any provision of this act is held invalid,
itshallnot affectin any way the other provisions of
the act,but they shallremain in fullforce and effect.
All laws and parts of laws in conflictherewith are
hereby repealed.
Approved March 14, 1924 .

CHAPTER 120 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , imposing an excise
or license tax on gasoline and all other liquid fuels ; including
all liquids ordinarily, practically and commercially usable in
internal combustion engines for the generation of power and
all distillates of , and condensates from petroleum , natural gas ,
coal, coal tar, vegetable ferments , and from all other products ,
so usable and sold in this Commonwealth at wholesale , as the
words " at wholesale " are defined in this Act ; provided the
term " liquid fuels " shall include kerosene , fuel oil and crude
oil only when used as a motor vehicle fuel upon any public
highway ; providing for the collection of such tax ; exempting
gasoline from local excise and license taxes ; the distribution
and use of the revenue derived therefrom , the administration
of said law , fixing fines and penalties for the violation of this
Act and repealing the present Gasoline Tax Law , which is con-
tained in Chapter ninety -three of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of nineteen hundred and twenty and Chapter ninety of
the Acts of the General Assembly of ninteen hundred and
twenty -two .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the word " gasoline," as used in this
act, shall include all liquidfuels, including liquids
ordinarily, practicallyand commerciallyusable in
internal combustion engines for the generation of
power and alldistillatesof,and condensatesfrom pe-
troleum, naturalgas, coal, coaltar, vegetablefer-
ments,and allotherproducts,so usableand soldin
this Commonwealth at wholesale, as the words " at

424 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
wholesale" are definedherein; provided, the term

crude oilonly when used as a motor vehiclefuelupon
any publichighway. A Statetaxof three(3c) cents
per gallonishereby imposed on allgasoline,as de-
finedherein, sold in this Commonwealth at whole-
sale, as the words " at wholesale" are hereinafter
defined . No other excise or license tax shall be lev-
ied or assessed on gasoline by the State or any
county,city,town or otherdistrict. The taxhereby
providedforshallbe paidby theperson,firm,asso-
ciationor corporationso sellinggasoline, and shall
be paid by saidperson,firm, associationor corpor-

the manner and within the time hereinafter speci-
fied. The words " at wholesale," as used in thisact,
shall be held and construed to mean and include any
and allsalesmade for the purpose of resaleor distri-
butionorforuse,and,as well,thegasolinefurnished
or supplied for distribution within this State,
whether the distributorbe the same person who so
furnished the same ,his agent or employer or another
person ; and also to mean and include any person
who shallpurchase or obtain such gasoline without
the State and sell or distribute or use the same with-
in the State. Provided , that nothing herein con-
tained shallbe construed as authorizing or requir-
ing the collectionof such tax upon any gasolineafter
the same shallhave been already once taxed under
the provisions of this act.
§2 . All taxes collectedunder the provisions of
this act shallbe paid into the State Treasury , and

reconstruction,maintenanceand repairof roadsand
highways ,and for the payment of the intereston and
forthecreationof a sinkingfund fortheliquidation

or cashier'scheck, payable to the State Treasurer

ceding calendar months .
§4 . If any such person, association,co-partner-

CHAPTER 122 .
AN ACT to provide for the execution and sale of Seventy -Five
Million Dollars of binds of the Commonwealth , fixing the time
when they shall be sold and the use of the proceeds thereof ,
providing for the application of public funds to the payment of
the principal and interest upon said bonds and to the mainten-
ance of roads , to the end that said bonds may be extinguished
within thirty years and said roads maintained in the mean-
while , and providing for the submission of this Act to the
voters at the general election to be held on November fourth ,
one thousand nine hundred and twenty -four .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssembly oftheCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . Bonds of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
shall be issued to the total amount of seventy-five
million dollars. Said bonds shall be of the denomi-
nation of one hundred dollars, or such multiples
thereof as the Commissioners of the Sinking Fund
of Kentucky may determine; shallbe engraved,
lithographed or printed as said commissioners shall
determine ; shallbe signed at the time of their re-
spectiveissuesby the Governor then in office, and
at the same time attestedby the Secretary of State
then in office, with the sealof the Commonwealth
affixed. All of said bonds shall bear interest at such
rate, not exceedingfiveper cent, as said commis-
sioners shall determine at the time of such issue.
All of said bonds shallbear date of July first, one
thousand nine hundred and twenty -five. The in-
tereston same shallbe payable semi-annuallyJan-
uary firstand July first,and thereshallbe attached
to saidbonds coupons for such semi-annualinterest
bearingthe facsimilesignatureof the presentGov-
ernor and of thepresentTreasurerof the Common-
wealth, the lastcoupon on each bond being due at
the date of maturityof the bond to which itisat-
tached. Said bonds and coupons shallbe payable

erationsellsor offersforsale,buys,or offerstobuy,

bleconsiderationisemployed eitherdirectlyor indi-
rectlyby a realestatebroker,to sellor offerto sell,

or saleor exchange of realestate,or to lease,to rent
or offerfor rent any real estate, or to negotiate
leasesthereon,or of the improvements thereon, as
a whole or partialvocation.
One actfora compensationor valuableconsidera-
tionof buying or sellingrealestateof or for another,
or offeringfor anotherto buy or sell, or exchange
realestate,or leasing,or renting,or offeringtorent
real estate, except as herein specificallyexcepted,

The provisionsof thisactshallnot apply to any

§3 . Creation of Commission - Details of Same .
There is hereby created a Kentucky Real Estate
Commission . The Governor shallappoint three per-
sons, whose vocationfor a period of at leastten
years prior to the date of theirappointment, shall
have been that of a real estate broker or a real estate
salesman;and who shallresidein a cityof the first
or second class; one member shallbe appointedfor
for a term of two years ; one member for a term of
threeyears ;and untiltheirsuccessorsare appointed
and qualified, thereafterthe term of the members
of said commission shall be for three years and
until their successors are appointed and qualified.
Members to fillvacancies shallbe appointed for the
unexpiredterm. The commission immediatelyupon
the qualificationof the member appointed in each
year shallorganizeby selectingfrom itsmembers
a chairman , and may do all things necessary and
convenient for carrying intoeffectthe provisions of
thisact and may from time to time promulgate nec-
essary rules and regulations.
Each member of the commission shall receive as

work of said commission the sum of ten dollarsper
day and hisactualand necessaryexpensesincurred

shallobtainsuch officespace,furniture, stationery,

The commission shalladopt a seal with such de-

thereon, by which itshallauthenticateitsproceed-
ings. Copies of allrecordsand papers in the office
of the commission,duly certifiedand authenticated
by the seal of said commission shallbe received in
evidence in allcourts equally and with likeeffectas
the original. All records kept in the officeof the
commissionunder the authorityof thisactshallbe
open to publicinspectionunder such rules and regu- .
lationsas shallbe prescribedby the commission .
All fees and charges collectedby the commission
under the provisionsof thisact shallbe paid intothe
generalfund of the State Treasury. All expenses
incurredby the commissionunder the provisionsof
thisact, includingcompensationto members ,secre-
taries,clerksand assistants,shallbe paid out of the

CHAPTER 139 .
AN ACT making an appropriation to the Kentucky Geological
Survey , and declaring an emergency .
Whereas , it has been shown that there are many good and
workable and commercially important deposits of road building
materials of various kinds and descriptions bountifully dis-
tributed in all of the counties of the State of Kentucky , and
Whereas there is now a very great need for a much larger
supply of the various road building materials as indicated by
the demands for construction by the Kentucky Department of
State Roads and Highways , and road contractors in and outside
of Kentucky , and
Whereas , there are many large and valuable unlocated , and
undeveloped deposits of minerals , ores and other substances
in the State of Kentucky adjoining and deposited with these
various road materials all of which are of great commercial
value , and
Whereas , information concerning same has never been ade-
quately prepared and is not now available, and
Whereas , the Kentucky Geological Survey has prepared a large
number of reports on various mineralogical and natural resource
subjects and a large number of manuscript maps all of which
are in the hands of the State printer but without funds with
which to publish them and place them in the hands of the people
of this State , in the interests of economy of road construction ,
and in the interests of making immediately available to the
people of this State these and other valuable mineral resources ,
reports and, maps pertaining to the hidden wealth of their prop-
erties, and thus advancing the general prosperity of the people
throughout the State , and
Whereas , much information concerning unused and unde-
veloped road materials and minerals can be prepared and fur-
nished by the Kentucky Geological Survey , and
Whereas manuscript reports and manuscript mape can be
published by the Kentucky Geological Survey with small im
mediate financial equipment ; therefore,

484 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That for the purpose of making such and
similar surveys as those indicated above through-
out the Stateand for theprintingand engravingof
such maps and reportsas shallproperly pertain
thereto and others now in the hands of the printer
and for such other generalpurposes of geological
survey administrationas may pertainthereto, in-
cludingifdesirablemapping or geologicalco-opera-
tion with the United States Geological Survey there
ishere appropriated the sum of seventeen thousand
dollarsper annum for the fiscalyears ending June
thirtieth, nineteenhundred and twenty-four, June
thirtieth,nineteenhundred and twenty-five,and June
thirtieth,nineteenhundred and twenty-six,from any
moneys in the State Treasury not otherwise appro-
priated, and in additionto the appropriationmade
by the Budget Act of ninteen hundred and twenty-
four.The Director and State Geologist with'the aid
of his assistantsshallwrite and publish the results
of these investigationsas soon as possible. The
printing and distributionof such maps and reports
tobe carriedoutby theKentucky GeologicalSurvey
as provided for in chapter thirty-four of the Acts of
nineteen hundred and twenty .
§2 . Since there are much data and much infor-
mation in the hands of the Director of the Kentucky
Geological Survey with relationto clays, road ma-
terials, buildingstones, fluorspar, coal, petroleum,
etc.,which would be of incalculablepresentuse and
benefitto the people of the Stateof Kentucky and
to the publicgenerally, in the form of fileddata,
manuscript reports and manuscript maps ,and since
itisimpossibleto furnish the persons entitledthere-
to with the information, reports and maps without

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 485
the necessary means for theirpublication,and since
Kentucky is being greatlyretardedin the proper
developmentofitsresourcesparticularlyroad build-
ing materialsof variouskinds by reason of a lack
of thisprepared and detailedinformation,an emer-
gency isdeclaredto exist,and thisAct shallbe and
become a law immediatelyupon itspassage.
Approved March 7, 1924 .
CHAPTER 140 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact section 3 of chapter 34
of the Acts of the General Assembly of Kentucky , 1920 session ,
touching the Kentucky Geological Survey .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That section3 of chapter 34 of the Acts of 1920,
entitled" An Act creatingthe Kentucky Geological
Survey, designatingits executiveofficerand his
duties, and providingfunds for itsmaintenance,"
be and the same is hereby repealed, and in lieu
thereof section 3 is re-enacted so as to read as fol-
lows :
§3 . Duties of Director and State Geologist- Re-
ports by . It shallbe the duty of the Director and
State Geologistto give his attentionto the adminis-
tration of the affairsof the Kentucky Geological
Survey ;to visitany and allpartsof the Stateso as
to make himself familiar with the needs of each
section; to supervise, outlineand edit the work of
his assistants;to advance the interestsof Kentucky
by presentingin person, or otherwise, before na-
tionalgeologicalmeetings,authoritativestatements
of the geologicaland mineral resourcesof the State;
and he may ,in his discretion, deliverone or more
lectures before educational institutions, upon the

486 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
subjectsdevelopedby the dutiesherein outlined;
and toundertakesuchfieldwork ashistimewillper-
mit,and perform such otherdutiesas may properly
pertaintohis office. He shallhave the supervision
oftheentirework ofthesurvey,and shallbe respon-
siblefor the accuracy of same . He shallmake a
briefreportbienniallyto the Governor on January
1st,attheconveningof theStateGeneralAssembly,
covering the activitiesaccomplishedby and pro-
posed for the Kentucky Geological Survey .
Under the direction and control of the Director
and State Geologist, the corps of the survey shall
proceed to make carefulgeological, geographical,
mineralogical,chemical,physical,soiland economic
surveys of the State; to enter upon record an ac-
curate statement of the extent of water power and
water courses, to locatecoal, oil, metaliferousde-
posits, clays, ores, building stones, etc., etc., to re-
port characteristicsand compositions of soilsand
minerals;tocollect,analyze,classifyand labelspeci-
mens of rocks, fossils, ores, coals, oils, gases,
minerals, soilsand other natural resources of the
State; to cause specimens of the above named ex-
hibitsof the State to be correctlylabeled and pre-
served in the museum of the State Universityat
Lexington and in the officeof the Geological Survey
at Frankfort .
The Directorand StateGeologistshallbe the
Curator of the mineralogicaland other collections
oftheKentucky GeologicalSurvey inthecustodyof
the Universityof Kentucky . As Curator of said
museum he shall arrange for the proper presenta-
tionand displayof saidmineralogicalcollectionsto
the general public.
Approved March 28 , 1924 .

CHAPTER 141 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Mrs. William Gilbert , and the infant
son of William Gilbert , whose husband , William Gilbert , lost
his life in the performance of a public service, and leaving
the said Mrs. William Gilbert as his widow .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssembly oftheCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
WHEREAS , William Gilbert was a guard at the
EddyvillePenitentiary,and losthislifewhilewith
other guards successfullypreventinga riotamong
prisonersthereinconfined;and,
WHEREAS , at the time of his death he was receiv-
ingcompensationofninety-five($95) per month and
left at his death a widow , Mrs. William Gilbert,
without property ; and ,
WHEREAS , the death of the said William Gilbert
was brought about by his performance of a public
service,and thereforethe appropriation hereinafter
made comes strictlywithintheterms ofsectionthree
of the ConstitutionofKentucky ;now ,be itenacted:
§1 . That the Auditor is directed to draw his
warrant upon the treasurer for the sum of two thou-
sand dollarsin favor of Mrs. William Gilbert, and
also draw his warrant in favor of the statutory
guardian of her infant son by William Gilbert for
one thousand dollars on the treasurer which sums
are hereby appropriated out of any money in the
treasurynot otherwiseappropriated.
§2 . This Act shall take effect from and after
itspassage .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

and also draw his warrant in favor of the statutory

nance,supervisionand controlof saidroad shallbe
subject to allthe terms and conditions set out and
providedfor in chapterseventeen(17),sectionfive
(5),Acts of1920,which isnow articletwo (2), sec-
tion4356e-5,Carroll'sStatutes,editionof 1922,es-
tablishingand creating a primary system of State
highways .

Mayfield , Kentucky , to the line between Kentucky and the State
of Tennessee , along the line of the Mayfield and Paris , Tennes-
see public road , a distance of twenty miles, and
Whereas , by the Act of the General Assembly aforesaid said
gravel road from Mayfield to the junction of said road and the
old State road between Murray and Hickman , and said old State
road from said junction to Murray were made a part of the
primary system of State highways of Kentucky , which gravel
road from Mayfield to the junction of said road with said State
road has been taken over and is now being maintained by the
Commonwealth as a part of its primary system of public high-
ways , and
Whereas , the State of Tennessee has completed and is now
maintaining its system of public highways from the line between
said states and from the south end of said gravel road built by
Graves county and Kentucky to Paris , Tennessee , and south
thereof , for a distance of forty miles , and
Whereas , it is of paramount importance not only to the ad-
joining counties of Kentucky and Tennessee , but also to the
general traveling public, that the section of said gravel road
from its junction with said state road south of the state line,
a distance of five and two-tenths miles , connecting with the
Tennessee system of highways , be made a part of the primary
system of State highways of Kentucky ; therefore,

CHAPTER 290 .
AN ACT to repeal and re-enact sections 1 , 2 and 3 respectively,
of chapter 17 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1920 ,
approved on the 16th day of March , 1920 , and which will be
found published in the Session Acts of 1920 , beginning on
page 76 thereof , all relating to roads and bridges and the
Department of State Roads and Highways , and creating a
State Highway Commission , fixing their compensation and
declaring an emergency .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That section1 of chapter 17 of the Acts of
1920,relatingto roads and bridges,be and the same
ishereby repealed and re-enacted, so that as re-en-
acted it will read as follows :
The department of the State government known
as the Department of Public Roads is hereby con-
tinued and shall be known and designated as the
Department of State Roads and Highways .
(1) There is hereby created a State Highway
Commission , which shall consist of four members
to be appointedfrom theirrespectivedistricts, all
of whom shallbe appointed by the Governor and con-
firmed by the Senate. Said commissioners shallbe
appointedfrom the two dominant politicalparties
and not more than two of whom shall be members of
the same politicalparty . Each of said commission-
ers shallbe appointed immediately upon the taking
effectof thisact, two of whom shallbe appointed
for a term of two years, and two of whom shallbe
appointed for a term of four years. Said term of
each to date from February 1,1924. The said two

596 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
members appointed from road commission districts
number 2 and number 3 as herein defined,shallserve
for a term of two years, or untilFebruary 1, 1926,
and the two members appointed from the firstand
fourth road commission district shall serve for a
term of fouryears,or up toFebruary 1,1928. Said
road commissioner districtNo. 1 shallbe composed
of thefirst,secondand trirdcongressionaldistricts;
road commissioner districtNo. 2 shallbe composed
of the fourth and fifthcongressionaldistricts; road
commissionerdistrictNo. 3 shallbe composed of the
sixth, seventh and eighth congressionaldistricts,
road commissioner districtNo. 4 shallbe composed
of the ninth,tenth and eleventhcongressionaldis-
tricts. On or before February 1, 1926, and every
two yearsthereafter,theGovernor shallappointtwo
members of said commission for a term of four
years, and in case of any vacancy or vacanciesby
resignation,death or otherwise,the Governor shall
fillthe same by appointment. The said commis-
sionshall,immediatelyafteritsappointment,or as
soon thereafteras practicable, meet and organ-
ize by selecting a chairman and secretary of
the commission from among its appointed mem-
bers and three appointed members shall constitute
a quorum forthetransactionof itsbusiness,and on
or beforeJuly 1, 1924, saidcommission shallmeet
and appointa StateHighway Engineer. The mem-
bers of said commission shallbe citizensand resi-
dents of Kentucky and shallnot be lessthan thirty
years of age,and each shallbe a representativebus-
iness man and be well informed as to the needs and
requirementsof differentsectionsof theState,with
referenceto roads and highways. The Governor
shall be ex -officioa member of the commission with-
out vote,and he shallnot be requiredto attendthe
meeting of thecommissionexceptat hisconvenience

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 597
or pleasure. No members of thecommission,State
Highway Engineer or any divisionengineer, or
other employe of the Department of Public Roads
shallhave any interestdirectlyor indirectlyin any
contractmade by the commission ,nor shallthey own
or have directlyor indirectlyany interestin the cor-
poration, firm,company , associationor partnership
engaged in the businessof mining, manufacturing,
preparingor sellingany road constructionmaterial,
or machinery, or engaged in the businesof building,
constructingor maintainingpublicroads,bridgesor
highways, or in furnishing, solicitingor executing
bonds for contractorsdealingwith the commission
or itsagents or officers. Before assuming the duties
of his officeeach member shall take the constitutional
oath of officeand a record thereof shall be entered on
the minutes of the commission . The said commission
shallhave its officewith the Department of State
Roads and Highways . The commission shallpro-
vide a book in which shallbe recorded allof itspro-
ceedings at each meeting , which records shall be
signed by the chairman and secretary of the board .
The commission shall meet at least once each month
and may meet at any time upon the callof the chair-
man or two members of the commission , such call
to be in writing and made a matter of record. It
shallbe the duty of the commission to make a care-
ful investigationof allproblems relatingto the con-
structionand maintenance of roads and bridges in
the State;itshallexamine allprojectsand ascertain
the feasibilityof allroutes and shallobtain informa-
tion as to the proper type or character of road for
any project; itshallexamine alltypes of road ma-
terialwhich may be used,or the use of which is con-
templated, in the construction, reconstructionor
maintenance of any road hereinafter mentioned ; it
shall,from timetotime,examine and have examined

598 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the books of the Department of State Roads and
Highways ; itshallgive the engineer fulland com .
pleteinformationas may be obtainedby it, of all
matters which itinvestigates,and shalladvisehim
fullyas to allmatters and thingsfor the best interest
of the State in the construction, reconstructionand
maintenance of roads and bridges, and the advice
and information given shall be made a matter of
record on the minutes of the commission . The com-
mission shallmake a report to the Governor at least
once each year, and oftenerif required by him , giv-
ing in detailallinformation which willfully advise
him and the General Assembly of the work done by
the department, and particularlyshowing in detail
all moneys expended and the purposes for which
spent. In reaching a conclusionon any matters per-
taining to the duties of his officethe engineer shall
consider, and reach his conclusionsfrom the infor-
mation and advice furnished him by the commission
and from information obtained by himself and as-
sistants from other sources . The commission shall

CHAPTER 291 .
SENATE RESOLUTION No. 5 .
Joint resolution for the benefit of Charley Wells , Bernard Wells
and Elbridge Wells , of Johnson county , Kentucky , to reim-
burse each of these parties for services rendered the Common-
wealth in one thousand nine hundred and ten .
Whereas , Charley Wells , Bernard Wells and Elbridge Wells
were each and severally awarded certificates of the second class
to teach in the public schools of Johnson county , Kentucky , by
the examining board of Johnson county , Kentucky ; and
Whereas , said certificateswere in full force and effect, and
when the services of no other teachers were available, the edu-
cational board of division number three , of Johnson county ,
Kentucky , at its regular meeting held on a day provided for by
statute for that purpose , entered into a contract with the said
Charley Wells , Bernard Wells and Elbridge Wells , employing
them to teach school as assistants in the schools of its subdis-
tricts numbers six, eight and nine , respectively, for and during
the term of six months , beginning on the eighteenth day of July ,
one thousand nine hundred and ten, and as compensation there-
for, they and each of them were to receive one -half of the pub-
lic money drawn by said districtfor said year ; and
Whereas , they and each of them did teach as assistants in
said schools for the term of six months , and monthly reports
thereof made , and their services as reported were accepted by
the trustees of said district by the chairman of said board of
education , and by the superintendent of the public schools of
Johnson county , Kentucky , and
Whereas , neither of the parties as aforesaid has received any
part of the compensation agreed upon for the services rendered
aforesaid, and which amounts to two hundred and thirty dollars
and eighty cents for Charles Wells , two hundred and thirty-seven
dollars and thirty -seven cents for Bernard Wells and two hun-
dred and forty-seven dollars and two cents for Elbridge Wells
with interest from that date as aforesaid ; and ,
Whereas , said amounts were not paid to said parties for the
technical reason that neither at the time of services rendered
held a certificate of that class which would have entitled either
to teach schools as principals; and
Whereas , said sums of money were repaid into the general
school fund of the State of Kentucky ; now , therefore :

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 609
Be it resolvedby the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That theAuditor of PublicAccountsishereby di-
rected to draw his warrants on the State Treasurer
in favor of McHenry Rhoads , Superintendentof
PublicInstruction,for the sum of two hundred and
thirtydollarsand eight cents and two hundred and
seventy-three dollarsand thirty-seven cents and two
hundred and forty-seven dollarsand two cents with
legalinterestas aforesaid,tobe charged tothe gen-
eral school fund of the State of Kentucky , and that
the saidsums be paid by thesaidSuperintendentof
Public Instructionto the said Charley Wells, Ber-
nard Wells and Elbridge Wells, respectively, as
money ispaid to teachersof thepublicschools.
Approved March 8, 1924 .

CHAPTER 293 .
SENATE RESOLUTION No. 16 .
A Resolution to authorize the Governor to appoint Commis-
sioners for the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the purpose
of conveying by deed a certain tract of land now owned by
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , located in the county of
Jefferson, in fee simple for the purpose of enabling the said
county to construct a road thereon from the Jefferson turn-
pike road to the tomb of the late President Zachary Taylor .
Whereas , the Commonwealth of Kentucky is the owner in fee
simple of the following described tract of land lying and being
in the county of Jefferson, to-wit : A tract of land in Jefferson
county , Kentucky , beginning at a stone near the Jefferson turn-
pike road in Mrs. John Shanks ' line and running thence with her
line N. thirty-three and three -fourths W. sixty-seven ninety -two
one -hundredths poles to a stake opposite the corner of the
Taylor graveyard , thence with the line of the graveyard three
sixty-four hundredths poles to a stake , thence S. forty E. fifteen
sixty-seven one hundredths poles to a stake , thence South thirty-
three and three -fourths E. fifty-two eighteenths poles to a stake
near said turnpike road , thence N. forty-one one -half E , two and
eight-tenths poles to the beginning containing ninety -three one-
hundredths of an acre , more or less. Having been conveyed to
the Commonwealth of Kentucky by deed from George McCurdy ,
of Jefferson county , Kentucky , party of the firstpart, to Messrs .
Hancock Taylor and A. Buford , Commissioners for the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , parties of the second part, dated the
tenth day of November , eighteen eighty-one , and recorded in the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 611
office of the clerk of the Jefferson county court in Deed Book
two hundred forty-five, page three hundred forty-three ; and
Whereas , certain citizens and residents of the said county of
Jefferson, including various clubs and civic organizations , lo-
cated therein,have expressed a desire that a road should be con-
structed, permitting suitable and proper access from the Jef-
ferson turnpike road to the tomb of the late President Zachary
Taylor ; and
Whereas , the County Commissioners of the said county of
Jefferson have agreed to construct the said road , provided that
the said county shall own the above described tract of land ;
now , therefore :
Be it resolvedby the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Governor ishereby authorizedto appoint
two reputablecitizensand residentsof the county of
Jefferson as Commissioners for the Commonwealth
of Kentucky ,to convey on behalf of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky to the county of Jefferson, all
thattractor parcelof land hereinabovedescribed,
in fee simple,to the County Commissioners of the
county of Jefferson,for the use and benefitof said
county,upon the express condition, which condition
shallbe the considerationexpressed in the deed of
conveyance , that the said county of Jefferson shall
constructand maintain at alltimes suitableroadway
upon saidtractofland,from theJeffersonturnpike
road to the tomb of the latePresident Zachary Tay-
lor,and providingthereinthatifand when thesaid
county of Jeffersonshallfailto constructand main-
tainthe said road, upon demand of the Governor,

CHAPTER 297 .
HOUSE RESOLUTION No. 4 .
Resolution for the benefit of William D. Overton .
!
Whereas , because of a resolution of the General Assembly
William D. Overton in firing a cannot in celebration of the an-
niversary of the birthday of the Irish Patriot, Robert Emmett ,
was dreadfully and permanently injured , as a result of which he
is a hopeless and helpless dependent cripple and invalid.
Be it resolvedby the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the sum of six hundred dollars an-
nually, payable fiftydollarsmonthly, be and the
same is appropriatedout of any moneys in the
Treasury not otherwiseappropriatedfor his relief.
The said sum to be paid monthly so long as the
said William D. Overton shall live.
Approved March 15 , 1924 .
